skill.name: technical-decision-record
skill.description: Helps teams structure, compare, and document technical decisions as Architecture Decision Records.
skill.summary: Guides teams through clear ADRs for technical and architectural choices.
skill.author: ChaiWithJai

security_audit.summary: Static findings were evaluated against the skill context and appear to be false positives. The reported backtick execution is a markdown fence, and the weak cryptography matches are ordinary prose or placeholders, not crypto use.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Fence Flagged as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged backticks at SKILL.md line 76. This line begins a markdown code fence for an ADR template and does not execute commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged syntax is a fenced markdown block marker. No executable shell or Ruby command context is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Pattern in Prose
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged weak cryptography at SKILL.md lines 3, 55, 96, 101, 106, and 161. These lines contain skill description text, ADR placeholders, or guidance prose, with no cryptographic implementation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines are documentation and template placeholders. I found no cryptographic APIs, algorithms, keys, or security-sensitive code.

content.value_statement: Technical choices are hard to track when context lives in chat or meetings. This skill turns decisions into structured ADRs with options, constraints, consequences, and review context.
actual_capabilities: Guides users through the main sections of an Architecture Decision Record.
Prompts for decision context, constraints, options, consequences, and reversibility.
Helps compare two to four technical options with pros, cons, and effort.
Identifies common ADR mistakes such as missing stakeholders or hidden tradeoffs.
Supports clear status tracking for proposed, accepted, and superseded decisions.
limitations: Does not validate technical claims against external sources by itself.
Does not create repository files unless the host tool performs that action.
Does not replace security, legal, compliance, or architecture board review.
Requires accurate user input about constraints, stakeholders, and options.

use_cases.title: Choose a Platform Component
use_cases.description: Compare databases, queues, frameworks, or managed services and record why one option was chosen.

use_cases.title: Document Team Architecture Changes
use_cases.description: Capture the context, constraints, stakeholders, and consequences behind an architecture shift.

use_cases.title: Create Decision History for Reviews
use_cases.description: Prepare ADRs that explain prior decisions for onboarding, audits, and future migration planning.

prompt_templates.title: Start a Basic ADR
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a single decision
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me write an ADR for choosing between these options: [options]. The decision is about [topic].

prompt_templates.title: Compare Options
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an ADR draft for [decision]. Include these constraints: [constraints]. Compare [option A], [option B], and [option C].

prompt_templates.title: Assess Consequences
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this decision idea and help me document positive consequences, negative consequences, risks, mitigations, and reversibility.

prompt_templates.title: Supersede an Existing ADR
prompt_templates.prompt: Draft a new ADR that supersedes ADR-[number]. Explain what changed, why the old decision no longer fits, and what migration work is required.

output_examples.input: We need to choose PostgreSQL or MongoDB for account data.
output_examples.output: An ADR draft with context, constraints, two options, a clear decision, expected benefits, accepted costs, risks, and reversibility notes.

output_examples.input: Our team wants to replace a REST integration with events.
output_examples.output: A structured decision record comparing REST, event streaming, and a hybrid approach, including affected stakeholders and migration consequences.

output_examples.input: We changed our build system last year and need to explain why.
output_examples.output: A retrospective ADR that records the original trigger, constraints, selected tool, rejected alternatives, and current follow-up actions.

best_practices: List at least two real options, including doing nothing when appropriate.
Record constraints and stakeholders before selecting the preferred option.
Create a new ADR when context changes instead of editing decision history.
anti_patterns: Using an ADR to justify a decision after ignoring alternatives.
Documenting only the selected option without risks or consequences.
Changing old ADRs instead of marking them superseded.

faq.question: What is an ADR?
faq.answer: An ADR is an Architecture Decision Record that explains a technical decision, its context, options, and consequences.

faq.question: Can this skill choose the best technology for me?
faq.answer: It helps structure the comparison, but the final decision depends on your facts, constraints, and expert review.

faq.question: Does it support superseded decisions?
faq.answer: Yes. The template includes status handling for proposed, accepted, and superseded ADRs.

faq.question: Can I use it for non-architecture decisions?
faq.answer: Yes. It can document process, tooling, service, and platform decisions when tradeoffs need a clear record.

faq.question: Does it write files automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides structure and content guidance. File creation depends on the AI tool and user permissions.

faq.question: Is it safe to use with confidential designs?
faq.answer: Use your organization's AI data policy. The skill itself does not include network calls or secret handling.
